content.problemStatement:
Kerim is an environment-friendly guy. Today, he accepted Samir's challenge of planting 20 million trees by 2020. Currently, there are NNN trees (numbered 111 through NNN) planted at distinct positions on a line; for each valid iii, the position of the iii-th tree is AiA_iAi​.

A set of trees is beautiful if for each tree in this set (let's denote its position by xxx), there is a tree at the position x−1x-1x−1 and/or a tree at the position x+1x+1x+1.

Kerim's task is to plant some (possibly zero) trees in such a way that the resulting set of all planted trees (the initial NNN trees and those planted by Kerim) is beautiful. It is only allowed to plant trees at integer (possibly negative) positions. Find the minimum number of trees he needs to plant in order to achieve that.
constraints: A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum number of trees Kerim needs to plant.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Kerim should plant trees at the positions 333 and 666 to make the grid beautiful, so the answer is 222.
